canonical_definition: Statistical test comparing model perplexity on public versus private watermarked rephrasings of the same content to detect dataset membership, using a one-sided test for lower perplexity on public versions.
papers: Prune ’n Predict: Optimizing LLM Decision-making with Conformal Prediction
STAMP Your Content: Proving Dataset Membership via Watermarked Rephrasings

definitions.text: Statistical test comparing model perplexity on public versus private watermarked rephrasings of the same content to detect dataset membership, using a one-sided test for lower perplexity on public versions.

definitions.text: A statistical procedure used to determine if there is a significant mean difference between two sets of paired observations, used here to assess the significance of accuracy changes.
definitions.paper: Prune ’n Predict: Optimizing LLM Decision-making with Conformal Prediction

relationships.evidence: “Using these perplexity scores, we perform a paired t-test to detect membership.”
